label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. So the question is, if I follow these steps, do I end up back where I started? The options are yes or no. Let me break down the instructions step by step. First, take 3 steps. Now, when they say "take 3 steps," I assume that's in a straight line. Let's say I start facing north. So, I go 3 steps north. Then, turn around. Turning around would mean 180 degrees, so now I'm facing south. So, if I was facing north before, now I'm facing south. Then take 5 steps. So, 5 steps south. So from my original position, I went 3 steps north, then 5 steps south. That would be a net movement of 5 - 3 = 2 steps south from the starting point. Then, turn right. If I'm facing south, a right turn would face me west. Wait, no. Let me think. If you're facing south and turn right, your right would be west? Wait, when you turn right, that's a 90-degree turn to the right from your current direction. So facing south, right turn would be west. Wait, no. Wait, if you're facing south and turn right (clockwise), you would be facing west. Wait, no. Wait, facing north, right turn would be east. Facing east, right turn is south. Facing south, right turn is west. Facing west, right turn is north. So yes, if I'm facing south and turn right, I'm facing west. Then another turn right. So now, from west, another right turn would be north. So after two right turns from south, I'm now facing north. Then take 1 step. Take 1 step. So, I'm facing north and take 1 step, then another step. So two steps north. So let's track the positions. Starting point: (0,0). Take 3 steps north: (0,3). Turn around (south), take 5 steps: (0,3 -5) = (0, -2). Then turn right twice (south to west then to north). Wait, no. Wait, after turning around (facing south), then turning right (first right turn) would face me west. Then another right turn (second right turn) would face me north again. So after those two turns, I'm facing north again. Then take two steps north: from (0, -2), moving 2 steps north (since north is the positive y-axis), so (0, -2 + 2) = (0,0). So I end up back at the starting point. So the answer would be yes. Wait, but let me double-check. Starting at 0,0. 3 steps north: 0,3. Turn around (south), 5 steps: 0,3-5=0,-2. Then turn right (south to west). Then turn right again (west to north). So now facing north again. Then take 1 step, then another step: total
